programs.name: Museum Adventure Pass
programs.tagline: Free museum admission through Lake County libraries
programs.description: Check out a Museum Adventure Pass from participating Lake County libraries and get free or reduced admission to dozens of museums and cultural attractions. Each pass admits a family for one visit.
how_it_works: Borrow a pass from your participating library's collection
Passes check out for 3–7 days depending on the library
Present the pass at the museum for free admission
Each pass covers the whole family (typically 2 adults + children)

featured_venues.name: Chicago Botanic Garden
featured_venues.note: Free general admission (parking fee still applies)

tips: Passes are popular — check out early in the week for weekend use
You can reserve passes online through most library catalogs
Some passes have blackout dates for special exhibitions

programs.name: Bank of America Museums on Us
programs.tagline: Free museum admission the first full weekend of every month
programs.description: Bank of America and Merrill Lynch cardholders get free general admission to 225+ museums, science centers, and cultural institutions — including several near Lake County — on the first full weekend of each month (Saturday and Sunday).
how_it_works: Program runs the first FULL weekend of each month (Sat + Sun)
Present your Bank of America or Merrill Lynch debit or credit card
Show a photo ID — admission is for the cardholder only (not whole family)
General admission only — special exhibitions may cost extra
tips: Admission is per cardholder — each adult with a BofA card gets in free
Weekends fill up fast at popular venues — arrive early or reserve timed tickets online
Check the BofA website each month — the venue list can change

programs.name: Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum — Free Community Days
programs.tagline: Free admission for Illinois residents on select Thursdays
programs.description: The Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum offers free admission for Illinois residents on Community Free Days (check current schedule). Home to a spectacular live butterfly haven, a river ecosystem exhibit, and hands-on nature activities for kids.
how_it_works: Check the museum website for current free day schedule
Show Illinois ID at the door
All general exhibits included; special exhibitions may cost extra

programs.name: Field Museum — Chicago Residents Free Days
programs.tagline: Free admission for Chicago residents and Illinois school groups
programs.description: The Field Museum offers free admission for Chicago residents on select days each year, and deeply discounted rates for Illinois residents. Home to Sue the T. rex and world-class natural history exhibits.
programs.location: 1400 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60605
how_it_works: Check the Field Museum website for current free day schedule
Chicago residents get the most free days; IL residents get discounts
Museum Adventure Pass can also provide free/reduced admission

programs.name: Brookfield Zoo — Free Admission Days
programs.description: Brookfield Zoo offers free general admission on select days throughout the year — typically a handful of times in spring, summer, and fall. Parking always has a fee. Check the zoo website for the current year's free days.
how_it_works: Check the Brookfield Zoo website for free day dates each year
Arrive early — free days are very popular
Parking fee still applies on free days (~$15–20)
Museum Adventure Pass may also provide free admission
